latestPosts.title: B-Trees and B+ Trees in Java: The Engines of Modern Databases
latestPosts.description: Understand the architecture of B-Trees and B+ Trees. Learn why these self-balancing trees are the standard for file systems and database indexing, and how they differ from Binary Search Trees.
latestPosts.excerpt: While Binary Search Trees (BST) and AVL trees are great for in-memory operations, they perform poorly when data is stored on disk. This is where B-Trees and B+ Trees shine. They are the underlying data structures for alm…

latestPosts.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java: Shortest Paths with Negative Weights
latestPosts.description: Master Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java for finding shortest paths in weighted graphs, including those with negative edge weights. Learn its intuition, implementation, dry runs, and how to detect negative cycles.
latestPosts.excerpt: Introduction to Bellman-Ford Algorithm Bellman-Ford Algorithm is a single-source shortest path algorithm that works on weighted graphs, including those with negative edge weights. Unlike Dijkstra's algorithm, which fails…

latestPosts.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java: Shortest Paths with Negative Weights
latestPosts.description: Master the Bellman-Ford algorithm in Java. Learn how it handles negative edge weights and detects negative cycles in a weighted graph, and how it compares to Dijkstra.
latestPosts.excerpt: While Dijkstra's algorithm is faster for most shortest-path problems, it fails when the graph contains negative edge weights. For those scenarios, we use the Bellman-Ford algorithm. Not only does it handle negative weigh…

latestPosts.title: Bloom Filters in Java: Probabilistic Data Structures
latestPosts.description: Master Bloom Filters in Java. Learn how this memory-efficient probabilistic data structure checks for set membership with zero false negatives and controllable false positives.
latestPosts.excerpt: A Bloom Filter is a space-efficient probabilistic data structure used to test whether an element is a member of a set. It is unique because it can give two types of answers: 1. "Possibly in set": The element might be the…

latestPosts.title: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm in Java: Finding the Majority Element
latestPosts.description: Learn the Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm in Java. Discover how to find the majority element in an array in linear time and constant space, a common and elegant interview favorite.
latestPosts.excerpt: Finding the "majority element" in an array (an element that appears more than $n/2$ times) is a classic interview problem. While you could solve it with a Hash Map in $O(n)$ time and $O(n)$ space, the Boyer-Moore Voting …
